cleanTargets.description: Temporary files created by applications and the current user session
Temporary files created by Windows services and system processes
Application launch prefetch files used to speed up program startup
Windows system and component log files including CBS and DISM logs
Log files from Windows installation and upgrade processes
Cached thumbnails and icons for File Explorer; rebuilt automatically on demand
Cached font rendering data; rebuilt automatically by the Font Cache Service
Compiled DirectX shader cache used to reduce game and app load times
Cached web content from Internet Explorer and Windows system components
Downloaded Windows Update packages that are no longer needed after installation
Cached update files used for peer-to-peer delivery optimization
Windows Error Reporting crash and problem reports for the current user
System-wide Windows Error Reporting crash and problem reports
User-mode application crash dump files
Kernel minidump files created during blue screen crashes
Cached MSI patch baseline files used for uninstall rollback
Archived Windows Event Log files (.evtx)
Historical scan results and detection records from Windows Defender
Files from a previous Windows installation kept for rollback; can be very large
Usage telemetry logs generated by the .NET Framework runtime (64-bit)
Usage telemetry logs generated by the .NET Framework runtime (32-bit)
Cached bitmap tiles from Remote Desktop sessions
Debug log files generated by Windows services such as Netlogon and WIA
Temporary files created by the Network Service system account
General-purpose caches used by Windows shell components

singleFileTargets.description: Complete memory dump file created during a system crash; often several GB
